project: DEATH GATE CYCLE - Match-3 Foundation Prototype

designPhilosophy.guidingPrinciple: Match-3 tests the pipeline for all systems we're building. Expandable to Death Gate realms.
designPhilosophy.futureExpansionPath: Four realms where gravity flips in each realm, Match-3 mechanics adapt

match3Rules.collisionDetection: Proper raycasts between tiles and catch zones

primaryGameInterface.description: Mobile-first Match-3 grid with spatial drag mechanics

behavior.displayType: 8×8 tile grid with parallax depth (tile Z-axis varies by level)
behavior.tileBehavior: Each tile has physical properties: weight, velocity, rotation inertia

dragMechanics.initialGrab: Pinch or touch-down on tile edge → Instant visual pickup
dragMechanics.midDrag: Tile maintains velocity vector + drag offset → Shows gravity influence
dragMechanics.snapThreshold: 0.5m radius around target position → Magnetic snap effect
dragMechanics.releaseBehavior: Drop at release point OR place in designated catch zone

behavior.displayType: Debug mode toggle + visual raycasts when interactable

raycastBehavior.activation: On drag start → Show raycast line from tile center to catch zone
raycastBehavior.lengthMax: Full screen diagonal for placement validation
raycastBehavior.feedback: Raycast endpoint glows when tile can snap to that position

collisionTypes.description: Prevents tile from being dropped outside grid bounds
Validates drop position within designated zones
Collision with special effect particles (line clear, explosions)

behavior.purpose: Ensures physics grounded interactions, prevents placement errors

behavior.displayType: Progressive bar with particle burst effects
behavior.animation: Score bar fills from left → Particle explosion at full score
behavior.matchPreview: 3-second countdown before match clears (prevents visual confusion)

thresholds.ready: 50% match timer → Strong pulse + haptic notification

behavior.displayType: Radii + colored glow zones where tiles can be caught/placed

states.approaching: Expand outward ring on drag start (scale: 1→1.5 over 300ms)
states.readyToCatch: Bright glow + directional arrow (tile ready to snap here)

physicsIntegration.catchZoneRadius: 8-12cm on mobile touch surface / 50m in VR space
physicsIntegration.snapBehavior: Magnetic pull to nearest valid grid position within catch zone
physicsIntegration.collisionPrevention: Raycast ensures tile won't clip through other tiles when dropped

behavior.purpose: Makes spatial interaction discoverable & precise, guides player placement

elements.name: Realm Indicator (Future Expansion)
elements.position: top-left (sky-space at 8m elevation when realms enabled)

behavior.purpose: Shows current realm physics state without HUD clutter (for future 4-realms)

elements.name: Primary CTA: Resume
elements.behavior: High contrast button with haptic confirmation on press

elements.name: Secondary Actions Column
elements.behavior: Tap to open sub-panel, no modal blocking

mobileFallbackMode.description: Portrait mode interface for phone-only/WebXR browser testing
mobileFallbackMode.layout: Single column, controls at bottom 40% of screen

controls.label: 🎯 Catch Zone Glow
controls.behavior: Expands on drag start, snaps when dropped within radius

primaryMovement.webXR: Tap-hold-drag sequence with visual grab feedback

dragToCatch.description: Physically grounded tile dragging with proper raycast collision

steps.action: Touch/pinch-down on tile edge → Instant visual pickup
Drag maintains velocity + gravity influence vector → Shows physics mode (normal/inverted)
Raycast validates catch zone position → Endpoint glows when valid placement available
Release within snap threshold (0.5m) → Magnetic pull to grid slot OR designated catch zone

visual.grabState: Tile lifts slightly off grid surface + glow color shift
visual.dragState: Trail particles follow velocity vector, show gravity influence (normal/inverted Y)
visual.snapPreview: Ghost tile preview in catch zone → Success checkmark on release

collisionDetection.description: Proper raycast validation between tiles and catch zones (prevents physics glitches)

types.raycastPurpose: Prevents tile from being dropped outside grid container
Validates drop position within designated zone radius
Collision with special effect particles (line clear, explosions)

activationConditions.alwaysActiveDebug: Raycast lines visible in debug mode / test builds
activationConditions.onInteraction: Raycast activates only during active drag → Optimizes frame budget

visualFeedback.activeDrag: #ff9100 (warning orange) line from tile center to catch zone endpoint
visualFeedback.validPosition: Endpoint glows bright → Shows where tile will snap on release
visualFeedback.invalidPosition: Raycast line fades out immediately when outside valid range

lineClear.description: 4+ tiles in row or column → Line particles burst outward
lineClear.animation: Particle wave expands 100ms, grid slots clear with fade
lineClear.feedback: Audio: Rising chime (800Hz → 1200Hz), Haptic: Strong pulse (75ms, magnitude: 0.5)

explosion.description: 5+ tiles in T/L shape → Radial explosion clearing all adjacent
explosion.animation: Explosion burst expands over 200ms → Debris particles scatter outward
explosion.feedback: Audio: Crackling burst (noise + rising pitch), Haptic: Heavy vibration sequence (3x pulses)

bombTile.description: 6+ tiles in L/T shape → Bomb activates clearing 3×3 area
bombTile.activationVisual: Bomb icon appears on center tile → Glows for 1s before detonation
bombTile.feedback: Audio: Loud boom sound, Haptic: Sustained vibration (200ms)

pauseInteraction.gestureDetail: Wave hand above shoulder ≥2m OR head shake (motor impairment)
pauseInteraction.palmOrientation: Must face camera for wave gesture

feedback.visual: Pause menu overlay fades in over entire screen

realmPryanInvertedY.physicsOverride: Tile gravity flips (drag upward → tiles fall up)
realmPryanInvertedY.gestureChanges: Visual indicator added to HUD → Shows inverted Y-axis active

realmChelestraLightVision.physicsOverride: Only matching tiles visible → Light beam highlights matches
realmChelestraLightVision.gestureChanges: Hold gesture activates light mode, release returns to normal vision

realmAbarrachSoundPatterns.gestureChanges: Wave direction determines sound type → Different frequencies for each color

features.implementation: Distinct tile textures + patterns (not just colors)

alternatives: Auto-drag mode: Tile moves to cursor location without manual drag
Extended grab time: 800ms hold-down instead of instant pickup
Simplified movement: Grid navigation via arrow keys (desktop mode)

ariaImplementation: <div role='grid' aria-label='Match-3 game grid, 64 points possible'>

unityIntegration.physicsEngine: Unity Physics with Raycast validation layer

day2: Mobile touch shell integration with raycast collision (Paithan)
day3: First integration demo with core match-3 mechanics
User testing session (5 participants, 15 min each)

futureExpansion.realmAdditionDay9: Light vision mode in Chelestra realm

phase1FoundationDays3_5.focus: Match-3 core mechanics + collision detection + raycast validation
phase1FoundationDays3_5.deliverable: Validated pipeline for particle effects, drag physics, grid UI

phase2aPryanGravityFlip.mechanics: Invert Y-axis gravity → Tiles fall upward instead of down
phase2aPryanGravityFlip.uiUpdates: Add realm indicator + visual physics mode toggle
phase2aPryanGravityFlip.gestureAdjustments: Drag direction feedback changes (drag up = tiles fall up)

phase2bChelestraLightVision.mechanics: Only matching tiles visible → Light beam highlights valid matches
phase2bChelestraLightVision.uiUpdates: Add light mode toggle + visual overlay effects
phase2bChelestraLightVision.gestureAdjustments: Hold gesture to activate light vision

phase2cAbarrachSoundPatterns.mechanics: Audio frequency-based tile identification → Each color has unique sound profile
phase2cAbarrachSoundPatterns.uiUpdates: Add audio toggle + sound visualization particles

phase2dArianusNormalGravity.uiUpdates: Default realm indicator shows normal gravity icon
